title: Direct RNA ISS- Phylotypic
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2i1g5","text":"Highly multiplexed spatial profiling of mRNAs has recently gained traction as a tool to explore cellular diversity, allowing to visualize the cell composition of tissues directly in-situ.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dtk1p","text":"This protocol exploits direct ligation of padlock probes on their target mRNAs, coupled with rolling circle amplification and hybridization-based in situ combinatorial barcoding, to achieve high detection efficiency, high throughput and large multiplexing (up to few hundred genes). The protocol borrows elements from SCRINSHOT (see https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33216742/) and Hybridization-based In Situ Sequencing (https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39099456/) for cheap and efficient detection of mRNAs in situ.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":333,"length":41},{"key":1,"offset":420,"length":41}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"39628CEDAB6611F089640A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33216742"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"58686239AB6611F089640A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39099456"}}}}
guidelines:
{"blocks":[{"key":"s6nr","text":"This protocol has been thoroughly tested on a variety of fresh frozen tissue sections coming from various animal models with minimal modification. Different tissues have sometimes slightly different cell or extra-cellular matrix composition and might need specific pre-treatments to make the tissue layers accessible to reagents and enzymes.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c467s","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5u93","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5lust","text":"Equipment","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5otk4","text":"
","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cofag","text":"Hydrophobic pen","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"elsf0","text":"Forceps","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9331p","text":"30°C, 37°C, and 45°C incubators","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d1ie5","text":"Humidity chamber for slide incubation","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5685k","text":"SecureSeal hybridization chambers (Grace Bio-Labs)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1husb","text":"Coverslips","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3h126","text":"Coplin jars or similar for washing of slides","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4i8aa","text":"Adhesive microscopy slides (e.g., Menzel Gläser SuperFrost Plus)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"all1b","text":"Wide-field epifluorescence microscope (6-channel)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bc3rg","text":"Plate rocker","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9lvj3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6vqv2","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e0e6c","text":"Necessary enzymes:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":18}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9sn93","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9ngee","text":"SplintR ligase","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":14}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"rhpe","text":"Phi29 polymerase","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bn1t1","text":"RNAse Inhibitor","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b3m6j","text":"
","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"evdih","text":"General Guideline and Controls","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":30}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"buv9h","text":"
","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bsh2r","text":"Enzymes and other reagents included in this protocol can be purchased from several different manufacturers (e.g. New England Biolabs or Thermo Fisher Scientific) and have performed equally well in our hands. Optimization, testing, and benchmarking should be performed whenever new reagents from different vendors are used. ","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"rkij","text":"Enzymes can be produced on the cheap by your local protein production facility, if you have one nearby. The patent rights for these enzymes have expired, we provide here links to the primary sequences for your convenience.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7ph6a","text":"Stock concentrations of reagents could vary depending on vendor used. Adjust tables so that final concentration of reagents is the same. Optimization might also be required.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cjbr4","text":"This protocol involves RNA work and special care needs to be taken to prevent RNases. It is recommended to have designated space and equipment for RNA work and should be treated/cleaned with commercially available RNase and DNAse inactivating agents and then wiping with 100% ethanol after treatment.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9a24i","text":"Using sterile, disposable, RNase-free plasticware (pipette tips, slide boxes, tubes, and  flasks) is recommended.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2ing","text":"PBS and water can be purchased as RNase free from numerous vendors. It is also possible to treat PBS and water with DEPC (diethyl pyrocarbonate) to make them RNase free.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fonse","text":"Synthetic DNA targets can be used to validate specificity of padlock probes. Rolling circle amplification (RCA) can be monitored in vitro by staining rolling circle products (RCPs) with either intercalating dyes (SYBR dyes) or decorator probes and visualized under a microscope or qPCR system.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fiec8","text":"This protocol assumes correct design of padlock probes (PLPs), L-probes, and detection oligos (DOs) for sequencing. See publications for further details on probe design to target genes of interest.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"694ae","text":"This protocol does not go into detail on padlock probe design and analysis of data. See publications for further detail and image analysis.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9ue20","text":"
","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"73q22","text":"Abbreviations","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"59t6r","text":"
","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9it2t","text":"PLP: padlock probe","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3i39","text":"RCA: rolling circle amplification","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"nbs5","text":"RCP: rolling circle product","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1vrie","text":"SBH: sequence by hybridization","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3gm4o","text":"DO: detection oligonucleotide","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"1r8rc","text":"Embed your fresh-frozen sample in a cryo embedding mold of your choice using OCT medium.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bg890","text":"Cut sections to a thickness of 10-20 micra and attach the sections to Superfrost slides.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f27cb","text":"Store the sections at -80 until use.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning:
{"blocks":[{"key":"56pfe","text":"See safety data sheets for proper chemical handling, precautionary measures and waste disposal.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2cce1","text":"Obey all local regulations/guidelines for handling and disposal of used reagents and and solutions containing reagents mixed in.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4i9ul","text":"
","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"87bsj","text":"Formamide:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3ambf","text":"Handle with proper attire including gloves and eye protection. Work under fume hood when handling solution and dispose of waste appropriately.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cs672","text":"Suspected of causing cancer.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a3ee2","text":"May damage fertility or the unborn child.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cejnn","text":"May cause damage to organs (Blood) through prolonged or repeated exposure if swallowed.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ejjv1","text":"
","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a1q9n","text":"Methanol:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9939b","text":"Handle with proper attire including gloves and eye protection. Work under fume hood when handling solution and dispose of waste appropriately.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"crivj","text":"Highly flammable liquid and vapor.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b9ap0","text":"Toxic if swallowed, inhaled or in contact with skin.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bdn4u","text":"Can cause damage to organs (Eyes, Central nervous system).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7kvk","text":"
","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3jj3v","text":"Formaldehyde/paraformaldehyde/formalin solution (PFA):","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dduot","text":"Handle with proper attire including gloves and eye protection. Work under fume hood when handling solution and dispose of waste appropriately.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8a17","text":"May cause cancer.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"35ajn","text":"Toxic if swallowed, in contact with skin or if inhaled.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cof65","text":"Causes severe skin burns and eye damage.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bu9jc","text":"May cause an allergic skin reaction.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f52q7","text":"May cause respiratory irritation.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"choc9","text":"Suspected of causing genetic defects.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2mvji","text":"Causes damage to organs (Eyes).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a88kq","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Fix with the freshly prepared 3,7% formaldehyde in PBS for 30 min at RT. 00:30:00, Tissue fixation Formaldehyde solution can be applied directly on top of the section, or the slide can be submerged in a large amount of fixative. Safety precaution: Formaldehyde Formaldehyde should be handled in a biosafety fume hood due to its hazardous nature.

Immerse the slide in the 1% SDS and incubate for 2 min at room temperature. 00:02:00, SDS Incubation This can be performed by submerging the glass slide in ~20 mL solution of 1% SDS in a jar or container that can hold slides.
Discard SDS solution and incubate in PBS at RT for 1 min 2 times.
Dehydrate the sections in order to aid the adhesion of SecureSeal Hybridisation chambers. Submerge slides in 70% Ethanol for 1 min. Transfer and submerge in ~100% Ethanol for 1 min.

Combine reagents for PLP hybridisations in table below. Reagent Stock Final Amount 1 20x SSC 20x 2x 5 μL 2 Formamide 100% 10% 5 μL 3 PLPs 1 uM each 10 nm each 0.5 μL 4 Nuclease-free water - - 39.5 μL 5 Total - - 50 μL PLP Hybridisation Mix Note: H2O is used to get total volume required. Other vendors can supply reagents at various stock concentrations and adjustments need to be made accordingly, including possible optimisation of concentrations. The amount of padlock probes will vary depending on user/experiment and initial concentration may vary. Final concentration of each padlock probe should be around 10 nM each. This may require optimisation depending on the total amount of padlock probe targets. PBS from chamber is removed and PLP Hybridisation Mix is added to the chamber. Incubate at 37 o C overnight. 37 °C Overnight, PLP Hybridisation Safety precaution: Formamide

Reagents for PLP ligation are combined as in the table below. Reagents Stock C Final C Amount 1 Nuclease-free water - - 40,25 μL 2 SplintR buffer New England Biolabs) 10x 1x 5 μL 3 RCA primer 1 μM 0.05 µM 2,5 μL 4 RiboProtect (RNase inhibitor) 40 U/μL 1 U/µl 1,25 μL 5 SplintR Ligase (New England BioLabs) 25 U/μL 0,25 U/μL 1 μL 6 Total 50 μL PLP Ligation Mix Note: H2O is used to get total volume required. Other vendors can supply reagents at various stock concentrations and adjustments need to be made accordingly, including possible optimisation of concentrations. PBS from chamber is removed and PLP Ligation Mix is added to the chamber. Incubate for 2 hours at 37 o C. 37 °C 02:00:00, PLP Ligation

Remove reagents from chamber after incubation. Add the wash buffer to the chamber and incubate for 1 min at 37 o C twice. 37 °C 00:01:00, Wash buffer incubation Wash twice in PBS. Note : Let PBS remain in chamber until next reagents are combined.

After overnight incubation, remove reagents from chamber. Wash chamber twice with PBS After washing steps, carefully remove SecureSeal chamber. Remove SecureSeal chamber with the aid of forceps. Wash the section with PBS one more time.

Reagents for RCA are combined as in the table below. Reagents Stock C Final C Amount 1 Nuclease-free water - - 34 μL 2 phi29 buffer 10X 1X 5 μL 3 glycerol (Placed at RT) 50% 5% 5 μL 4 dNTPs 25 mM 0,25 mM 0,5 μL 5 BSA 20 μg/uL 0.2 µg/mL 0,5 μL 6 phi29 polymerase 10 U/μL 1 U/µL 5 μL 7 Total 50 μL RCA Mix Note: H2O is used to get total volume required. Other vendors can supply reagents at various stock concentrations and adjustments need to be made accordingly, including possible optimization of concentrations.
Take the slides from -80°C and leave them at room temperature (RT) for 3-5 min to air dry and reach room temperature. 00:03:00, Thaw at RT
Discard formaldehyde from tissue section and incubate in PBS at RT for twice. For the next washing steps, submerge the glass slide in ~20mL PBS in a jar or container that can hold slides.
Item Stock C Final C Volume 1 Formaldehyde 37% 3,7% 2 mL 2 PBS 0,1M - - 18 mL 3 Total - - 20 mL 3,7% Formaldehyde preparation
Prepare the buffers for the fixation and permeabilisation steps.
Item Stock C Final C Total volume 1 SDS 10% 1% 2 mL 2 Nuclease-free water - - 18 mL 3 Total - - 20 mL 1% SDS preparation

Item Stock C Final C Volume 1 Methanol 100% 70% 14 mL 2 Nuclease-free water - - 6 mL 3 Total - - 20 mL 70%Methanol preparation NOTE: Chill the Methanol solution in the freezer (-20) during the next steps and until needed.

Immerse the slide in the pre-chilled 70% Methanol solution. Incubate for 60 min on ice. On ice 01:00:00, Methanol permeabilisation The whole container should be submerged in ice. Safety precaution: Methanol Methanol should be handled in a biosafety fume hood due to its hazardous nature.
Discard methanol solution and incubate in PBS at RT for 1 min 2 times.
Remove slide and let air dry (1-5 min)

With a dry surface around tissue section, apply appropriate size SecureSeal Hybridisation chamber over the tissue. SecureSeal Hybridisation chambers come in different sizes, shapes, and depths. Small chambers have a volume of ~50 µl (round, 9 mm diameter, and 0.8 mm deep: enough for half a coronal section of a mouse brain). For larger tissue specimens, larger chambers and shapes can be used and consequently volumes in protocol should be adjusted. Chambers have to be mounted on dry surfaces for a proper seal to prevent reagent content loss during incubations.

Sections and chamber are washed by applying PBS-T to chamber inlet and then followed by one wash of PBS. Note : Let PBS remain in chamber until next reagents are combined. Apply and remove solutions to chamber by tilting the slide/chamber slightly and pipetting into lower inlet. This will aid in preventing bubble formation within the chamber.

Prepare wash buffer. Reagent Stock Amount 1 Nuclease-free water - 400 μL 2 Formamide 100% 50 μL 3 SSC 20X 50 μL 4 Total - 500 μL Safety precaution: Formamide Preheat the wash buffer at 37 o C for 10 mins. 37 °C 00:10:00, Wash buffer preheat
Wash twice with PBS. Note : Let PBS remain in chamber until next reagents are combined.

Fresh frozen tissue samples are embedded in OCT (optimal cutting temperature) compound and stored at -80°C until cryosectioning. Tissue is cryosectioned at 10 μm thickness and collected on SuperFrost Plus adhesion slides and can be stored at -80°C until used for experiment. Extra precautions should be taken with tissue of human origin. Obey local guidelines/rules and take appropriate precautionary measures when handling tissue, especially fresh tissue to avoid any risks.
Remove reagents from chamber after incubation.
PBS from chamber is removed and RCA mix is applied to the chamber. Incubate at 30 o C overnight. 30 °C Overnight, RCA

Then carefully remove as much liquid as possible from area surrounding the tissue section. Use paper towel to dry area if needed. Then, with a hydrophobic pen, draw a barrier surrounding tissue section. After the barrier has been drawn around tissue and dried slightly, apply additional PBS to section to prevent it from drying out and proceed to next step. We use ImmEdge Hydrophobic Barrier PAP Pen by Vector Laboratories (Cat. No. H-4000). Other hydrophobic pens have shown to impede the in situ sequencing visualisation. It is also possible to reapply SecureSeal hybridisation chambers instead of using hydrophobic pen for subsequent hybridisation steps. We find it easier to continue all steps without the use of the chamber. Note that the same volume should be used on tissue sections as what the chamber volume was.

Combine reagents for L-Probe hybridisation as in table below. Reagents Stock C Final C Amount 1 Nuclease-free water - - 22,6 μL 2 4X Hybridisation buffer 4Χ 1Χ 25 μL 3 L-Probe mix 2,083 μΜ 0,1 μΜ 2,4 μL 4 Total - - 50 μL L-Probe Mix Note: H 2 O is used to get total volume required. Various stock concentrations are possible and adjustments need to be made accordingly, including possible optimisation of concentrations.

Prepare 4X Hybridisation Buffer according to the table below. Reagents Stock Amount 1 Nuclease-free water - 100 μL 2 Formamide 100% 100 μL 3 SSC 20X 50 μL 4 Total - 250 μL 4X Hybridisation Buffer NOTE: Keep the hybridisation buffer when not using.

PBS is removed from section and is washed once with 2X SSC. L-probe hybridisation mix is added to the section. Incubate L-probes Mix for 1h at RT in dark, on a rocker. Room temperature 01:00:00, L-probe incubation
Discard reagents and wash 2 times with 2XSSC. Let 2XSSC remain until next step reagents are prepared.

Combine reagents for detection oligo hybridisation as in table below. Reagents Stock C Final C Amount 1 Nuclease-free water - - 22,25 μ 2 4X Hybridisation Buffer 4X 1X 25 μL 3 DO_seq1 10 μg/μL 0,1 μg/μL 1 μL 4 DO_seq2 10 μg/μL 0,1 μg/μL 1 μL 5 DO_seq3 10 μg/μL 0,1 μg/μL 1 μL 6 DO_seq4 10 μg/μL 0,1 μg/μL 1 μL 7 DO_seq5 10 μg/μL 0,1 μg/μL 1 μL 8 DAPI 100 μg/μL 0,5 μg/μL 0,25 μL 9 Total - - 50 μL Detection Oligo Mix Note: H 2 O is used to get total volume required. Various stock concentrations are possible and adjustments need to be made accordingly, including possible optimisation of concentrations.
2XSSC is removed from the tissue. L-probe hybridisation mix is added to the section. Incubate L-probes Mix for 1h at RT in dark, on a rocker. Room temperature 01:00:00, Detection Oligo incubation
Discard reagents and wash with PBS 2 times. Let PBS remain until TrueView reagents are prepared.

Mix equal parts of TrueView reagents, by first mixing reagents A and B together and then adding reagent C. Vortex well in between each addition and spin down the mix. Discard the PBS from the tissue. Add the TrueView mix on the tissue and incubate for 1 min at RT in dark. Room temperature 00:01:00, TrueView incubation
Discard the TrueView mix and wash the tissue with PBS three times.

Remove as much PBS as possible and then add small drop of mounting media and apply cover slip to slide section. We use SlowFade Gold Antifade Mountant as it has shown to perform well with our conditions and also allows for removal of cover slip in future steps. 10 μl of mounting media should be enough for the area of one mouse brain coronal section. Slides at this point can be stored at +4°C with mounted coverslips in the dark for several weeks. It is recommended to image sections soon after detection oligos are added.

Section can now be imaged. Labs will have different microscope set ups and identical setups are not needed. Fluorophore dyes can be interchanged depending on your own microscopes capabilities. The number of detection oligo sequences presented here is 5, but this is only limited by your microscopes ability to distinguish fluorophores and can be adjusted to be more or less which would also require adjustments to your combinatorial decoding scheme. We use a Leica DMI6000 epifluorescence microscope. It is equipped with 6-channel LED light source and sCMOS camera. A good filter setup is essential to provide good wavelength separation and minimal crosstalk between different channels. A typical imaging run includes DAPI for nuclei and 5 separate fluorophores for the different detection oligo sequences.

Imaging is set up to do repeated cycles of the same region every round. Tile region outlines are set up for the first cycle and saved. After every round/cycle, slides are placed back in stage slide holder to the same position for proper alignment. This is aided with the use of an automatic stage that can calibrate to a reference point every time it is used so tile regions have the same coordinates.
Submerge slide with coverslip in clean PBS in a coplin jar and let sit for a few minutes. Proceed to next step during this time and prepare reagents.

While slide is submerged, prepare stripping solution according to the table below. Reagents Stock C Final C Amount 1 Nuclease-free water - - 25 μL 2 Formamide 100% 65% 65 μL 3 SSC 20X 2X 10 μL 4 Total - - 100 μL Stripping solution Safety precaution: Formamide Pre-incubate combined reagents and humidity chamber at 30°C until used. 30 °C, Stripping solution pre-incubation Note: After incubation of section with stripping solution, section is washed once with additional stripping solution, therefore prepare twice as much needed. Prepare 2XSSC washing solution. For 5 ml: 500 μl 20XSSC + 4.5 ml Nuclease-free water
Remove slide from PBS and if cover slip does not slide off by itself, either let it sit longer in PBS or carefully slide it off with the aid of forceps.
Wash section 2 times with PBS to remove residual mounting media.

Place slide in prewarmed humidity chamber. Apply prewarmed stripping solution on top of section and place in 30°C incubator for 30 min. 30 °C 00:30:00, Stripping incubation Place any remaining stripping solution in the incubator as well.
After 30 min, remove solution from section and wash 1 time with remaining stripping solution.
Wash 5 times with 2XSSC.
Now the L-Probes and Detection Oligos has been stripped from the tissue. From this point, the next cycle can be performed as before with next cycle of L-probes.
creator: Christina Bekiari
